title: Dashlane vs Passbolt for Power users
meta_description: Dashlane vs Passbolt for power users who want self-hosted password management. See why Passbolt wins when infrastructure control matters.
constraint_lens: Power users prefer tools that can run inside their own infrastructure with full administrative control.

one_second_verdict.summary: Best for power users who want to deploy a password manager inside their own server infrastructure.
one_second_verdict.reason: Dashlane fails first because it cannot be deployed on a self hosted server under full administrative control.

verdict.summary: Passbolt is the better choice for power users managing credentials inside internal systems. It can be deployed on a self hosted server where administrators control the infrastructure and storage. Dashlane operates as a vendor hosted password manager and does not allow running the service inside a private environment. For users who need infrastructure level control, that hosted model limits what they can deploy.
verdict.decision_rule: If the password manager cannot be deployed on a self-hosted server environment under full administrative control, Dashlane fails first.

sections.heading: Why Passbolt fits power users
sections.content: The user manages credentials for internal systems and wants to deploy the password manager inside their own infrastructure. Passbolt runs on a server controlled by the organization and integrates with internal authentication and network systems. Administrators manage the environment directly instead of relying on an external service. This allows full control over how credentials are stored and shared.

bullets.point: Passbolt can be installed on a self hosted server environment.
bullets.why_it_matters: Power users can run the password manager inside their own infrastructure instead of relying on a hosted service.

bullets.point: Passbolt provides administrative control over the server and credential storage.
bullets.why_it_matters: Organizations can control how passwords are stored, backed up, and secured.

bullets.point: Passbolt integrates with internal systems and authentication infrastructure.
bullets.why_it_matters: Teams can connect password management to their existing internal tools and workflows.

bullets.point: Dashlane automatically synchronizes the password vault across devices through the user account.
bullets.why_it_matters: Users can access credentials quickly without managing server infrastructure.

bullets.point: Dashlane browser extensions detect login forms and autofill credentials.
bullets.why_it_matters: Signing into websites becomes faster without copying passwords manually.

bullets.point: Dashlane manages infrastructure and updates as part of the hosted service.
bullets.why_it_matters: Users do not need to maintain server software or handle system updates.

items.fails_when: The user wants a password manager that works instantly without maintaining server infrastructure.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Dashlane where the service is hosted and managed automatically.

items.fails_when: The user needs to deploy the password manager inside their own infrastructure because Dashlane cannot run as a self hosted server.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Passbolt where the password system can be deployed inside a private server environment.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: If the user prefers a hosted password manager that works immediately without running server infrastructure, Dashlane may become the better option.

sections.heading: Quick decision rules
rules: Pick Passbolt if you want to deploy the password manager on your own server.
Pick Passbolt if you need full administrative control over credential infrastructure.
Pick Dashlane if you want a hosted password manager with no server maintenance.

faqs.q: Why do power users prefer Passbolt?
faqs.a: Passbolt can be deployed on a self hosted server where administrators control the infrastructure.

faqs.q: Can Dashlane run on a private server?
faqs.a: No. Dashlane operates as a hosted password manager and does not support self hosted deployment.
Yes. Passbolt is typically installed on a server environment controlled by the organization.
Users who want a password manager that works immediately without running server infrastructure may prefer Dashlane.
